attention1.ptxt: When the discharge headlight lights up, high voltage is present at the igniter, which may cause electric shock.
When performing service operations around discharge headlights, be sure to observe the following precautions:

item.ptxt: Disconnect negative (–) cable at battery, and remove headlight connectors.
Do not perform service operations in a place exposed to water including rain or with wet hands.
Never attempt any measurement with tester connected to ballast connector or igniter.
Headlight bulb is hot while it is lit or immediately after it is turned off. Touching a hot bulb may cause a burn.
When handling a bulb, wait until it has cooled down.
Headlight bulb contains high pressure gas. If it is damaged and broken, scattered glass may cause injury.
When handling a bulb, be careful not to damage it.
If you touch the glass surface of a bulb with your bare hand, the oils from your skin can cause the bulb to overheat and burst when the lights are on.
Do not touch any bulb when removing the headlight housing.
Replace headlight bulb only with replacement bulb of correct specifications and wattage.
Check lighting of headlight bulb only with all headlight components assembled in position and headlight unit installed on vehicle.
Used bulb contains toxic substances and should be disposed of properly as industrial waste without breaking it.

servinfosub.title: Ballast Diagnosis
servinfosub.ptxt: When the ballast is exposed to excessive shock, there is a possibility that the ballast is broken even if the discharge headlight is lit up.
Check the lighting conditions of headlights as follows. If no unstable condition such as flickering is noted, the ballast is normal.

item.ptxt: Keep the headlights off for 10 minutes or more. Then, keep them on for about 5 minutes.
Keep the headlights on for 15 minutes or more and off for 1 minute. Then, keep them on and off for short time several times. After that keep them on.
